events.title: 🌷 AI Workshop for Content Creators (Spring 2026)
events.tagline: Get real results from your content with AI-powered workflows.

speakers.name: Bill Raymond
speakers.title: Host
speakers.shortBio: Bill is a technical content strategist and AI practitioner who uses generative tools daily to ship human-centered documentation and learning experiences.

faq.question: Do I need prior AI experience to join?

blocks.text: No prior AI experience is required. This training is designed for beginners and is still useful for experienced participants who want a more reliable workflow.

faq.question: Do I need anything before the workshop starts?

blocks.text: By the time you take this course, you must have a paid AI account from ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Microsoft 365 Copilot.

faq.question: How is the workshop delivered, and what if I miss a session?

blocks.text: Sessions are hosted live on Zoom. If you miss a session, there are no official makeup dates, but Bill accommodates reasonable scheduling-conflict requests when possible and shares the best available catch-up option.

faq.question: What will I get by the end (certificate, exercises, and take-home work)?

blocks.text: You will complete hands-on exercises, receive practical take-home materials, and earn a digital certificate of completion after presenting your final project.

faq.question: How can I ask questions before enrolling, and what if I can’t make this cohort?

blocks.text: If you have questions before enrolling, use the Cambermast contact form. If this cohort timing does not work, we can share upcoming options on the events calendar.

faq.question: 📧 Do you have a manager budget-approval letter I can send?

blocks.text: Yes. Use the email template below, open it in your email client, or copy it with one click.

blocks.subject: Request for approval: AI training program
blocks.body:
Hi [Manager Name],

I would like approval to attend [Program Name] with Cambermast.

This training is focused on practical, responsible AI use and includes
hands-on exercises I can apply directly in my role. I expect this will
help me improve speed and quality in [team/process area], while following
our policies and quality standards.

Program: [Program Name]
Dates: [Dates]
Cost: [Cost]
Link: [Program/Event URL]

After the training, I will share key takeaways and specific ways we can
apply what I learned to our team.

Thank you for considering this request.

Best,
[Your Name]

description.summary: Across weekly 2-hour sessions, participants combine strategy discussions with hands-on practice to automate repetitive tasks, preserve brand voice, and publish higher-impact content faster.
description.bodyMd:
The AI Workshop for Tech Writers and Content Creators is a live, seven-week cohort that helps technical communicators and content teams weave generative AI into their daily production workflows.

## Outcomes
- Automate repetitive content production tasks and accelerate delivery timelines.
- Tailor generative AI outputs to match established voice, tone, and terminology.
- Connect content decisions to audience journeys, measurable outcomes, and ROI.

## What You Will Build
- Persona-driven workflows that use AI tools to surface gaps and opportunities.
- Ready-to-use prompt libraries, custom GPT instructions, and evaluation checklists.
- A documented project plan that operationalizes AI-assisted content production.
- Digital certificate of completion once the Week 7 project is presented.

## Who This Is For
- Technical communication and documentation specialists modernizing their workflows with AI.
- Content strategists responsible for aligning deliverables to user journeys and business goals.
- Knowledge teams supporting structured and unstructured content across the enterprise.

highlights: Automate repetitive content production tasks with AI
Preserve brand voice with reusable prompts and guardrails
Ship a final project and earn a completion certificate
audienceBullets: Technical communication and documentation specialists modernizing their workflows with AI.
Content strategists responsible for aligning deliverables to user journeys and business goals.
Knowledge teams supporting structured and unstructured content across the enterprise.
buildBullets: Persona-driven workflows that use AI tools to surface gaps and opportunities.
Ready-to-use prompt libraries, custom GPT instructions, and evaluation checklists.
A documented project plan that operationalizes AI-assisted content production.
Digital certificate of completion once the Week 7 project is presented.
outcomes: Automate repetitive content production tasks and accelerate delivery timelines.
Tailor generative AI outputs to match established voice, tone, and terminology.
Connect content decisions to audience journeys, measurable outcomes, and ROI.

agenda.title: Week 1: Adopting the AI Mindset
details: Explore AI foundations for content teams and align on ethical guardrails.
Map quick wins that remove repetitive production work.
Practice prompt basics for clarity, structure, and factual rigor.

agenda.title: Week 2: Mapping Your Audience Journey
details: Build personas and user journeys that guide AI-augmented content.
Align messaging and assets to each audience touchpoint.
Test AI tools that simulate reader questions and expectations.

agenda.title: Week 3: Personalization at Scale
details: Deliver platform-specific versions without sacrificing consistency.
Automate tone, style, and format adjustments with controlled prompts.
Design custom GPT instructions to replicate your brand voice.

agenda.title: Week 4: Accelerating Value Delivery
details: Streamline production workflows and remove bottlenecks.
Run AI-powered content audits to prioritize improvements.
Plan projects and tasks that increase overall content ROI.

agenda.title: Week 5: Deep Content Insights
details: Identify gaps, new opportunities, and research angles with AI.
Develop outlines and drafts for net-new assets.
Define the project you will evolve through Week 7.

agenda.title: Week 6: Data-Driven Prioritization
details: Use customer insights to prioritize content that converts.
Apply advanced prompting techniques to sharpen clarity.
Review progress on your individual or team project.

agenda.title: Week 7: Evolving with AI
details: Present final projects and gather peer plus mentor feedback.
Codify a long-term roadmap for AI-enabled content operations.
Operationalize your workflow and celebrate the cohort completion.

events.title: 🪄 AI Workshop: Code and Automate with ChatGPT Codex (April, 2026)
events.tagline: A five-week hands-on cohort for coding and automation with ChatGPT Codex.

faq.question: Do I need prior AI experience to join?

blocks.text: Yes, some prior AI experience is recommended for this workshop. It is designed for builders, operators, consultants, IT professionals, and aspiring developers who already want to use AI to build and improve practical apps and automations.

faq.question: What do I need before the workshop starts?

blocks.text: Complete the full setup guide before Week 1. You should have Visual Studio Code, the ChatGPT Codex extension, Git, GitHub, Docker Desktop, and a paid ChatGPT account ready to use. You should also have a laptop, stable internet, and a project idea or workflow you want to turn into a practical app or automation.

blocks.label: Open the AI Workshop: Code and Automate with ChatGPT Codex setup guide

faq.question: Do I need prior coding experience to join?

blocks.text: No formal developer background is required. This workshop is designed for semi-technical participants, including IT professionals, aspiring developers, and people who want guided practice building practical apps or automations.

faq.question: Is this workshop tied to one coding product or platform?

blocks.text: This version is centered on ChatGPT Codex so everyone learns the same coding and automation workflow. The habits still help you evaluate other tools later, but the live exercises use ChatGPT Codex.

faq.question: What kinds of projects can I build or automate during the cohort?

blocks.text: Most participants build a practical app, internal tool, or workflow automation tied to work they already care about. That could include an operations dashboard, intake form, workflow helper, internal utility, or another lightweight product you want to keep improving after the workshop.

faq.question: Will we cover Git, GitHub, and Docker?

blocks.text: Yes. Week 3 introduces Git and GitHub for version control and collaboration, plus Docker for creating a consistent development environment. The goal is practical familiarity so you can keep improving your app with a more professional workflow.

faq.question: How does the capstone project work?

blocks.text: You will shape your capstone as the workshop progresses, using the exercises and weekly feedback to improve it over time. In Week 5, you finalize the project, present what you built, and leave with clear next steps for continuing development after the cohort ends.

faq.question: Will we use ChatGPT Codex planning features to make the app more robust?

blocks.text: Yes. In Week 4, the workshop focuses on using ChatGPT Codex planning features to break larger goals into manageable steps, improve the structure of your app or automation, and make the project easier to extend, test, and maintain.

faq.question: 📧 Do you have a manager budget-approval letter I can send?

blocks.text: Yes. This version is tailored to AI Workshop: Code and Automate with ChatGPT Codex and auto-fills the course title, dates, cost, and link from the training program details below.

blocks.subject: Request for approval: [Program Name]
blocks.body:
Hi [Manager Name],

I would like approval to attend [Program Name] with Cambermast.

This training is focused on coding, building automations, and improving practical tools with ChatGPT Codex. It covers hands-on development, Git, GitHub, Docker, ChatGPT Codex planning features, and a capstone project I can apply directly to my role.

Program: [Program Name]
Dates: [Dates]
Cost: [Cost]
Format: [Format]
Link: [Program/Event URL]

I expect this training will help me improve speed, quality, and technical confidence while giving me a more structured workflow for building useful internal or client-facing tools.

Thank you for considering this request.

Best,
[Your Name]

description.summary: In this five-week workshop, you will start with a prompt and progressively design, build, enhance, and refine practical apps and automations with ChatGPT Codex. Each session combines short learning sessions with hands-on building. By the end of every week, you will have something working that you can continue improving. By week five, you will have a practical project deployed from your own code repository or workflow environment. If you have some prior software experience and are ready to build, you are a strong fit for this workshop.
description.bodyMd:
You already understand software development concepts, and you are ready to bring your ideas to life. Now you need the skills to turn those ideas into working software and useful automations whenever you decide it is time to build. ChatGPT Codex helps teams create real apps, internal tools, and workflow automations through structured collaboration with AI. Learning best practices and strong prompting techniques is the new essential skill for modern builders and operators.

## Outcomes
- Follow proven development practices to ensure ChatGPT Codex-generated code remains reliable and maintainable.
- Make your application or automation easier to deploy with Docker.
- Prevent accidental breaking changes and keep your code safe using Git and GitHub.

## What You Will Build
- A repeatable workflow for planning, building, testing, and improving code with ChatGPT Codex.
- Prompt patterns and review habits that help you get better coding results.
- A practical app, internal tool, or automation you can continue improving after the workshop.
- A competitive market analysis of your competitors to support product positioning and planning.
- Greater confidence using AI to support technical work while staying grounded in ChatGPT Codex.
- A digital certificate of completion after presenting your final project.

## Who This Is For
- Builders who want to move faster with ChatGPT Codex coding workflows.
- Operators looking to create internal tools, apps, and automations.
- Consultants who want to prototype solutions for client and internal work.
- IT professionals exploring practical ways to use AI for coding and technical problem solving.
- Aspiring developers who want guided practice building with AI.

highlights: Turn plain-language ideas into practical apps and automations
Enhance a website, then use GitHub and Docker to iterate reliably
Ship a capstone project and earn a completion certificate
audienceBullets: Builders who want to move faster with ChatGPT Codex coding workflows.
Operators looking to create internal tools, apps, and automations.
Consultants who want to prototype solutions for client and internal work.
IT professionals exploring practical ways to use AI for coding and technical problem solving.
Aspiring developers who want guided practice building with AI.
buildBullets: A repeatable workflow for planning, building, testing, and improving code with ChatGPT Codex.
Prompt patterns and review habits that help you get better coding results.
A practical app, internal tool, or automation you can continue improving after the workshop.
A competitive market analysis of your competitors to support product positioning and planning.
Greater confidence using AI to support technical work while staying grounded in ChatGPT Codex.
A digital certificate of completion after presenting your final project.
outcomes: Follow proven development practices to ensure ChatGPT Codex-generated code remains reliable and maintainable.
Make your application easier to deploy with Docker.
Prevent accidental breaking changes and keep your code safe using Git and GitHub.

agenda.title: Week 1: Build Your First Web App
details: Understand AI-assisted coding and how it works.
Explore common ChatGPT Codex coding workflows.
Practice writing clear requirements to translate into working code.
Build a simple working app or automation and review the output.

agenda.title: Week 2: Enhance Your Website
details: Improve the layout, content, and interaction flow of your Week 1 website.
Use ChatGPT Codex to add or refine pages, sections, forms, and visual polish.
Practice iterative prompting when the first AI output is incomplete or incorrect.
Review changes carefully so the website becomes clearer, more useful, and easier to maintain.

agenda.title: Week 3: GitHub, Docker, and Reliable Iteration
details: Use Git and GitHub to track changes, collaborate, and ship reliably.
Use Docker to create a repeatable development environment for your app or automation.
Improve your website through structured iterations and versioned commits.
Practice recovering from mistakes without losing working code.

agenda.title: Week 4: Planning for a More Robust Product
details: Use ChatGPT Codex planning features to break product ideas into manageable development tasks.
Refactor your app or automation into clearer modules and components that can scale.
Add validation, edge-case handling, and basic tests.
Deploy your working project.

agenda.title: Week 5: Capstone Project and Next Steps
details: Present your project or next big idea with the cohort.
Share ideas, tools, or workflows you want to continue exploring.
Leave with a clear path for turning future ideas into working apps with ChatGPT Codex.

events.title: Practical AI at Work: Create Your Own AI Work Companion
events.tagline: Spend one hour. Get one valuable result.
events.summary: In this live one-hour session, Bill Raymond shows you how to design and create a custom animated pet for Codex.
events.description: You will establish one approved main look and develop it into a reusable Codex pet package. The pet is a visual companion rather than another AI assistant.
highlights: Turn a description or reference image into a clear character design
Choose visual details that remain readable when the pet is small
Create one approved main look that anchors every later pose
Translate the character into animations without losing its identity
Review motion and correct poses that drift or do not read clearly
Package the finished design as a custom Codex pet

events.title: Practical AI at Work: Build an Automated Inbox Hero
events.tagline: Spend one hour. Get one valuable result.
events.summary: In this live one-hour session, Bill Raymond shows you how to use ChatGPT Work to build an Inbox Hero for unread Gmail messages.
events.description: You will build a reusable workflow that marks unimportant messages as read while keeping messages that need your attention unread. Nothing is archived, deleted, sent, or unsubscribed automatically.
highlights: Have AI identify routine, low-value messages and mark them as read automatically
Keep customer questions, security notices, decisions, and other important messages unread
Reduce inbox clutter so the messages that need you stand out
Spend less time sorting routine updates manually
Leave ambiguous messages unread for human review
Refine the rules as you learn what deserves your attention

events.title: Practical AI at Work: Turn Your Brand Guide Into an AI-Ready Skill
events.tagline: Spend one hour. Get one valuable result.
events.summary: In this live one-hour session, Bill Raymond shows you how to organize your brand into a structured AI skill.
events.description: You will build a minimum viable brand skill and refine it using a real branded visual. The session is for people responsible for recurring branded work who already have approved materials and want a more reliable way to use them with AI.
highlights: Write copy and content in a consistent voice
Build branded landing pages
Design social images and event graphics
Create presentations, reports, and decision visuals
Review AI-generated work against approved brand guidance
